Title: Roles of three cytochrome P450 monooxygenases in triterpene biosynthesis and their potential impact on growth and development

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pentacyclic triterpene scaffolds derived from lupeol/lupanediol
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides triterpene scaffolds upstream of CYP-mediated oxidation; co-silencing with NaOSC2 reduced β-amyrin, erythrodiol, and oleanolic aldehyde in floral tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previous functional characterization cited; VIGS co-silencing; metabolite profiling; heterologous expression context in prior work
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlated with NaCYP716C87 in tissue expression analyses
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Competitive growth/nutrient limitation in VIGS assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NaOSC1/2 cosilencing reduced rosette diameter but did not markedly reduce capsule number
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data from Nicotiana attenuata Data Hub
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary subject in this paper was not NaOSC1 itself, but it served as an essential upstream scaffold gene and positive VIGS control
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaOSC1 is an upstream triterpene scaffold-forming gene previously characterized in N. attenuata. 2. In this study it was mainly used for correlation analysis and as part of NaOSC1/2 VIGS controls. 3. Co-silencing with NaOSC2 reduced endogenous floral triterpenes.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. attenuata 31st-generation inbred line; N. benthamiana NA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies β-amyrin scaffold for downstream CYP716A419/C87/E107 oxidation; co-silencing with NaOSC1 reduced β-amyrin, erythrodiol, and oleanolic aldehyde in flowers and β-amyrin in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS co-silencing in Nicotiana attenuata; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with NaCYP716A419, NaCYP716E107, and NaCYP716C87
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Competitive growth/nutrient limitation in VIGS assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NaOSC1/2 cosilencing reduced rosette diameter but did not markedly reduce capsule number
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data from Nicotiana attenuata Data Hub
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used extensively as the scaffold-producing enzyme in transient expression assays to reveal downstream P450 activities
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaOSC2 is the upstream β-amyrin synthase-type scaffold gene used to functionally reveal CYP716 activities. 2. It enabled production of β-amyrin-derived oxidized triterpenes in N. benthamiana. 3. Co-silencing with NaOSC1 lowered endogenous triterpene accumulation in N. attenuata.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. attenuata 31st-generation inbred line; N. benthamiana NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential 3-step C28 oxidation of β-amyrin, lupeol, and lupanediol, converting the C28 methyl to alcohol, aldehyde, and carboxylic acid products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: erythrodiol; oleanolic aldehyde; oleanolic acid; betulin; betulinic acid; 28-hydroxy lupanediol; 28-aldehyde lupanediol; 28-carboxy lupanediol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate intermediates and downstream acidic triterpenes in the pentacyclic triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation step after oxidosqualene cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; biologically important downstream oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Oleanolic acid; betulinic acid; maslinic acid-related triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized metabolites with ecological bioactivity; likely roles in growth/development and defense
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers (corolla, pollen, theca); roots under AMF colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of C28-oxidized triterpenes; silencing increases β-amyrin and decreases erythrodiol/oleanolic aldehyde in flowers and attenuates growth, pollen viability, and capsule production in N. attenuata
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS with authentic standards; substrate feeding; VIGS silencing in Nicotiana attenuata; metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers, followed by roots, lowest in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In N. attenuata: highly expressed in flowers, followed by roots, lowest in leaves; induced in roots by ABA, SA, GA3, MeJA, and alkaline stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA; SA; GA3; MeJA; PEG6000; NaCl; Na2CO3; arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi colonization; competitive growth/nutrient limitation in VIGS assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes reduced rosette diameter, stalk diameter/height, flower bud abortion, lower pollen viability, and fewer seed capsules; possible toxicity from accumulated intermediates or loss of downstream compounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering oxidized pentacyclic triterpenes such as oleanolic acid/betulinic acid derivatives, but perturbation may impose strong growth and reproductive penalties, so deployment requires caution and likely pathway balancing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data from Nicotiana attenuata Data Hub
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Products were validated in heterologous N. benthamiana; in N. attenuata clear metabolite changes were detected mainly for erythrodiol and oleanolic aldehyde in flowers, while additional products were detected in AMF-colonized roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaCYP716A419 is a functional CYP716A C28 oxidase acting on β-amyrin, lupeol, and lupanediol. 2. It sequentially generates alcohol, aldehyde, and carboxylic acid derivatives including erythrodiol, oleanolic acid, betulin, and betulinic acid. 3. Silencing in N. attenuata reduced C28-oxidized floral triterpenes and caused strong defects in growth and reproduction.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. attenuata 31st-generation inbred line; N. benthamiana NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C2α hydroxylation branch of β-amyrin/lupeol/lupanediol-derived triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the C2α position of β-amyrin, erythrodiol, oleanolic acid, lupeol, lupanediol, betulinic acid, and putative 28-carboxy lupanediol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: β-amyrin; erythrodiol; oleanolic acid; lupeol; lupanediol; betulinic acid; putative 28-carboxy lupanediol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 2α-hydroxy β-amyrin; 2α-hydroxy erythrodiol; maslinic acid; 2α-hydroxy lupeol; 2α-hydroxy lupanediol; alphitolic acid; putative 2α-hydroxy-28-carboxy lupanediol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms downstream hydroxylated triterpene intermediates and maslinic acid-type products
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation/hydroxylation step after scaffold formation and after/parallel to C28 oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots under AMF colonization; not detected in normal flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables C2α-hydroxylated triterpene formation including maslinic acid, but silencing in N. attenuata caused little detectable effect on measured endogenous floral triterpenes or reproductive output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic inference; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; combinatorial coexpression with NaOSC2/AtLUP1 and NaCYP716A419; GC-MS with authentic maslinic acid standard; VIGS in Nicotiana attenuata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers and roots, low in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In N. attenuata: high in flowers and roots; strongly induced in roots by ABA, SA, GA3 and PEG6000, reduced by NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA; SA; GA3; MeJA; PEG6000; NaCl; Na2CO3; AMF colonization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing showed no major capsule-number defect and no significant change in major detected floral triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for engineering maslinic acid/2α-hydroxylated triterpenes, likely requiring suitable upstream scaffold and/or specific inducing conditions to achieve accumulation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data from Nicotiana attenuata Data Hub
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatically active in heterologous assays, endogenous products were mostly undetectable except maslinic acid in AMF-colonized roots, suggesting condition-specific accumulation or rapid downstream metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaCYP716C87 is a CYP716C C2α hydroxylase. 2. It accepts multiple pentacyclic triterpene alcohols/acids and forms maslinic acid and related hydroxylated derivatives. 3. Its endogenous products in N. attenuata appear condition dependent and were detected notably in AMF-colonized roots.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. attenuata 31st-generation inbred line; N. benthamiana NA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C6β hydroxylation branch of β-amyrin/oleanolic acid-derived triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-amyrin at C6β to form daturadiol and hydroxylates oleanolic acid to putative 6β-hydroxy oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: daturadiol; putative 6β-hydroxy oleanolic acid; incompletely derivatized 6β-hydroxy oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream oxidation/hydroxylation step after scaffold formation and after/parallel to C28 oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots under AMF colonization; not detected in normal flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces C6β-hydroxylated triterpenes from β-amyrin and oleanolic acid; silencing caused modest growth reduction and increased oleanolic aldehyde in corolla but limited endogenous metabolite effects were detected
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; comparison with SlCYP716E26; substrate feeding; combinatorial coexpression with NaOSC2/NaCYP716A419; GC-MS; VIGS in Nicotiana attenuata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers, followed by roots, lowest in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In N. attenuata: highly expressed in flowers and roots; induced by ABA, GA3, MeJA, PEG6000 and strongly by Na2CO3; reduced by NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA; SA; GA3; MeJA; PEG6000; NaCl; Na2CO3; AMF colonization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced rosette diameter and stalk height somewhat, but no major reproductive penalty was reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering C6β-hydroxylated triterpenes, but activity is weaker than SlCYP716E26 on β-amyrin and product accumulation may require matching substrate context and environmental induction
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray expression data from Nicotiana attenuata Data Hub
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NaOSC2; NaCYP716A419 (pathway coexpression/combination context); SlCYP716E26 used as comparator
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows weaker activity on β-amyrin than tomato SlCYP716E26 and did not act on lupeol/lupanediol or maslinic acid in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaCYP716E107 is a CYP716E C6β hydroxylase. 2. It converts β-amyrin to daturadiol and oleanolic acid to putative 6β-hydroxy oleanolic acid. 3. Endogenous products were mainly detectable under AMF-colonized root conditions, indicating conditional accumulation.
